title: Deaths due to unsafe sex
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to [unsafe sex](#dod:unsafe_sex). Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.
caption:
Deaths due to unsafe sex

Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex. Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.

This choropleth world map displays the estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex in 2023. The legend uses a color scale ranging from light beige for values near zero to dark red for values up to 300,000, with specific thresholds marked at 1,000, 3,000, 10,000, 30,000, and 100,000. The highest death counts, represented by the darkest red shades exceeding 100,000, are concentrated in South Africa and India. China and Russia appear in the next darkest band, falling between the 30,000 and 100,000 range. A broad cluster of countries, including the United States, Brazil, Mexico, and nations across Eastern Europe and Southeast Asia, sit in the mid-range orange bands, generally between 3,000 and 30,000 deaths. Much of Western Europe, Canada, and Australia display lighter shades, indicating values below 3,000. Large portions of North Africa and the Middle East also appear in these lighter bands. Greenland and several smaller island territories are marked with a hatched pattern, indicating no data is available for these regions. The visual distribution highlights a significant concentration of deaths in Southern Africa and South Asia, with moderate numbers across the Americas and Eurasia, and lower counts in Oceania and Western Europe.
